text: Six trocars are positioned, including three 12mm and two 5mm trocars. A right-side trocar is utilized to place external brachytonicae, retracting the hepatic hilum to expose the caudate process and Spiegel lobe. The surgeon dissects the left triangular ligament to mobilize the left lateral segment of the liver.

The surgeon identifies an accessory left hepatic artery and opens the gastropathic ligament to expose the corded lobe. An external tourniquet is placed for the Pringle's maneuver, retracting the hepatic hilum to the patient's right side. An intraoperative ultrasound is used to visualize and delineate the tumor limits.

The surgeon begins the transaction on the medial side of the Spiegel lobe, encountering small branches of the left portal vein. The section continues with blunt detachment of the vena cava in the posterior plane of the Spiegel lobe. The ligament to the diaphragmatic cross is sectioned to separate the lobe from the vena cava. Alternating between medial and posterior approaches, the dissection progresses. The gastrohepatic ligament, cranial to the left accessory hepatic artery, is opened to improve mobilization of the Spiegel lobe. Liver transsection is completed shortly thereafter.

The surgical specimen is removed through an enlarged port on the left side. The surgeon inspects the surgical site for hemostasis and applies a fibrinogen and thrombin sealant. The final view shows the port used for the external tourniquet and the enlarged port for specimen removal. Negative margins are confirmed.
